title: Maestro Handoff Packet
description: Formalizes the AGENTS.md S4/S6 cross-talk and checkpoint packet: the minimum context routed between specialists, or persisted before spawns, handoffs, and resumes. Optional tooling aid: the prose doctrine remains the source of truth. Before acting on a compressed packet, verify objective, scope, criteria, risks, and next action are present (S8).

objective.description: What the receiving agent must accomplish or what the checkpoint preserves.

files.description: Files in scope, with roles if helpful (e.g. 'src/api.ts -- interface changed').

requirements.description: Constraints and acceptance criteria still in force.

decisions.description: Decisions already made, each with its rationale. Receiving agents do not re-litigate.

risks.description: Known risks, cross-breakage candidates (interfaces/imports/types/state), unverified assumptions.

next_action.description: The single next step. A resume starts here, not from full history (S6).

upstream_changes.description: Cross-talk payload (S4): what an earlier specialist changed that affects the receiver -- minimum context only (signature, not diff).
